title: Escape sequences should use the delimited form (\u{}, \o{}, \x{})
description: Escape sequences can be used to designate a single character literal or characters in a string literal. Escape sequences followed by a character code are especially handy when the character is non-visible or is not directly representable in the source code encoding. However, before C++23 the way to write them is convoluted.
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

checks.message: Use delimited escape sequences (\x{}, \o{}, \u{}, \U{}) instead of traditional forms for better readability and consistency.
